sutta: The Buddha said monks if for just the lasting of a finger snap the monk indulges the thought of mettā here translated as good will such a one is to be called among not empty of result is his jhāna he abides doing the master's bidding he is one who takes good advice and he eats the country's arms food to some purpose what then should I say of those who make much of such a thought.
commentary: Here the Buddha is trying to say that even good will or loving-kindness or impersonal love universal love if you even have uh a partner of mettā then that manga is really is called a real monk and here the Buddha also say it's not empty of result it is jhāna so from here it would appear that to practice mettā you have to attain janafus to attain jhāna then only you can radiate thismettāl now.
